commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR sanitizes TTS summary-model output in `summarizeText` and adds focused unit and contract tests for reasoning tags, control tokens, prompt echoes, truncation, and preservation edge cases.

PR surface: Source +128, Tests +193. Total +321 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows a PR-head input that echoes the full prompt before `<text_to_summarize>` leaves the leading `You are an assistant...` instructions after tag-block removal, and current main already returns raw summary-model text to the auto-TTS caller.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can update the summary sanitizer and tests for the remaining full-prompt echo case on this PR branch.

target.title: fix(tts): sanitize summarized speech text
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90364.

TTS summarization now cleans summary-model output before speech synthesis:

- reuses the shared assistant-visible text sanitizer
- removes complete and EOF-truncated echoed `<text_to_summarize>` prompt blocks
- strips only prompt-specific leading summarizer preambles while preserving valid first-person and user-request summary prose
- preserves usable summary content after colon/dash prompt preambles without dropping subject-bearing user-summary clauses
- caps the sanitized summary at the requested speech target length

## Root Cause

`src/tts/tts-core.ts` joined `TextContent` blocks from `completeSimple` and returned that raw string as `summary.summary`. Auto-TTS then assigned that value directly to `textForAudio`, so reasoning tags, model control tokens, prompt echo, or echoed original text could be spoken.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Long-response TTS summaries no longer pass hidden reasoning/control scaffolding, echoed summarization prompt text, complete or truncated echoed `<text_to_summarize>` content, or control tokens through to the audio text candidate. The review feedback cases are also covered: legitimate first-person summary prose is preserved, valid summaries starting with `The user asked me to summarize ...` are preserved before periods and separators, usable content after bare colon-style prompt preambles is preserved, and truncated prompt blocks are stripped through EOF.

Real environment tested: Dedicated local macOS OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-tts-summary-sanitization` at commit `30e4da3b98`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Evidence after fix: Terminal capture from the local OpenClaw source run:

```console
$ pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts
OpenClaw TTS summary proof
summary="Concise audible summary."
outputLength=24
targetLength=120
leakedForbiddenTokens=none
audioText=Concise audible summary.
```

Observed result after fix: The audio text candidate was `Concise audible summary.`. The proof input included summarizer preamble, `<think>` reasoning, `<|assistant|>` control token, tool-call XML, and an echoed `<text_to_summarize>` block; the produced audio text candidate had `leakedForbiddenTokens=none` and stayed under the requested target length.

What was not tested: Live Microsoft/MiniMax audio playback was not run because provider credentials and audio playback setup were not available in this local checkout.

Proof limitations or environment constraints: This proof exercises the production `summarizeText` cleanup path from the PR worktree with controlled provider text. It verifies the text handed toward speech synthesis, not the downstream Microsoft audio renderer.

## CI / Review State

- Review comments from ClawSweeper and Codex on earlier heads were addressed through `30e4da3b98` by tightening prompt-echo detection, preserving first-person summary prose, preserving valid `The user asked me to summarize ...` summary prose before periods and separators, preserving content after bare colon/dash prompt preambles, and stripping EOF-truncated `<text_to_summarize>` echoes.
- GitHub workflows are running on the current PR head; current live status is available in the PR Checks tab. Local focused verification below passed on `30e4da3b98`.
- Codex autoreview was attempted before PR creation, but local sandbox escalation for the external Codex review service was rejected, so this PR relies on focused local proof plus GitHub CI/review.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` in the dedicated worktree to hydrate dependencies
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tts/tts-core.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/tts.contract.test.ts packages/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-utils.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-helpers.sanitizeuserfacingtext.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec tsx /private/tmp/openclaw-tts-summary-proof.ts`

Focused verification passed across 3 Vitest shards: 2 unit-fast files / 165 tests, 2 unit files / 50 tests, and 1 plugin-contract file / 42 tests.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
